浅.origin: Water 氵 on the left means shallow water — like a stream you can wade across without getting wet.

sentence.en: The little river's water is very shallow.

正.origin: A foot 止 stopping right on the line 一 — standing straight and squarely in the correct spot.

sentence.en: His answer is correct.

突.origin: A dog 犬 suddenly bursts out of its cave 穴 — surprise! That's how 突 means sudden.

sentence.en: It suddenly started to rain hard.

松.origin: The tree 木 on the left shows this is a kind of tree — the tall, green pine tree.

sentence.en: There are many pine trees on the mountain.

鼠.origin: A whole mouse drawn out — teeth on top, paws on the left, and a long curly tail to the right.

sentence.en: The cat is chasing a mouse.

淹.origin: Water 氵 on the left — so much water that it covers everything over and drowns it.

sentence.en: The flood drowned the fields.

定.origin: A person settling down to stay under a roof 宀 — once they sit, the plan is decided.

sentence.cn: 我们定好了明天去公园。
sentence.en: We have decided to go to the park tomorrow.

猴.origin: The animal sign 犭 on the left tells you this is a creature — the cheeky, climbing monkey.

sentence.en: Monkeys love to eat bananas most.

捞.origin: A hand 扌 on the left does the work — reaching down to scoop and dredge something up from the water.

sentence.en: He scooped a fish up out of the water.

群.origin: Sheep 羊 on the right — and sheep love to gather, so 群 means a whole group or flock.

sentence.en: A flock of birds flew across the sky.

啊.origin: A mouth 口 on the left shows it is a sound we say — 'ah!' when surprised or happy.

sentence.en: Ah, there's a rainbow in the sky!

接.origin: A hand 扌 on the left reaches out to join things together or to catch what comes your way.

sentence.en: Please catch this ball.

碰.origin: A rock 石 on the left — think of two stones bumping and touching each other, knock-knock.

word.en: to bump into, to run into

sentence.en: I bumped into a classmate on the road.

司.origin: A mouth 口 giving orders inside a curved shape — the boss who takes charge and manages things.

sentence.en: Dad works at a company.

扑.origin: A hand 扌 on the left springs forward — pouncing and striking quickly, like a cat on a mouse.

sentence.en: The kitten pounced on the little ball.

缸.origin: The pottery sign 缶 on the left shows this is a clay vessel — a big round jar or fish tank.

sentence.en: There are two goldfish in the fish tank.

装.origin: Clothes 衣 sit at the bottom — 装 is what you wear, your outfit, and also how you dress things up.

sentence.en: She is wearing beautiful clothes.

吓.origin: A mouth 口 on the left lets out a cry — 'boo!' That sudden shout is how 吓 means to scare.

sentence.en: This story gave me a fright.

哭.origin: Two mouths 口 like crying eyes above, with a little dog 犬 below — boo-hoo, lots of tears.

sentence.en: Little brother fell down and started to cry.

慌.origin: The heart sign 忄 on the left — when your heart races and feels wild, that's feeling panicked.

sentence.en: Don't panic, speak slowly.

法.origin: Water 氵 flows along its own course 去 — a steady path to follow, just like a rule or law.

sentence.en: I thought of a good method.

使.origin: A person 亻 on the left carrying out a task — sent to do a job and make things happen.

sentence.en: Please use the scissors correctly.

劲.origin: Strength 力 on the right shows this is about power — the energy and force you push with.

word.en: to exert effort, to use strength

sentence.en: He pushed the big door open with all his might.

砸.origin: A rock 石 on the left — drop a heavy stone down hard and you smash and crush things.

sentence.en: The ball smashed into the window.

靠.origin: Picture two people back-to-back, leaning on each other for support — that's what 靠 means.

word.en: to draw near, to lean close

sentence.en: Please do not get close to the fire.

仰.origin: A person 亻 on the left tips their head way back — looking up high to admire something.

sentence.en: He looked up at the stars.

颗.origin: Fruit 果 on the left, head 页 on the right — 颗 counts small round things, like a seed or star.

sentence.en: There is one bright star in the sky.

连.origin: A cart 车 rolling along the road 辶 — moving and linking one place to the next, joining them up.

sentence.en: This bridge connects two villages.

勺.origin: A curved scoop 勹 with a little something 丶 held inside it — that's a spoon ready to feed you.

sentence.en: Drink the soup with a spoon.

斗.origin: Picture a hand gripping a long-handled dipper for measuring grain — a tool that turned into 'fight'.

sentence.en: The warrior fought bravely.

离.origin: Picture a strange animal standing tall, head 亠 on top — over time 离 came to mean to leave or be apart.

sentence.en: We leave school at three o'clock.

衡.origin: A big 大 horn 角 sits inside the road sign 行 — think of weighing and balancing things just right.

sentence.en: He kept his balance on the wooden bridge.

晓.origin: The sun 日 on the left is just peeking up — early light at daybreak, when day begins to dawn.

word.en: to know, to be aware of

sentence.en: As soon as dawn broke, the birds began to sing.

孟.origin: A child 子 sitting above a bowl 皿 — picture the eldest, the first child who leads the others.

sentence.en: The weather in early summer is very warm.

浩.origin: Water 氵 on the left — imagine a huge, wide ocean stretching far. 浩 means vast and grand.

sentence.en: The sea is vast and mighty.

眠.origin: An eye 目 on the left — when your eyes close and rest, that is sleep.

sentence.en: Good sleep is very important.

啼.origin: A mouth 口 on the left makes the sound — the cry of a baby or a bird at dawn.

word.en: to crow, to cry out (of birds)

sentence.en: The rooster crows early in the morning.

悯.origin: The heart sign 忄 on the left — when your heart feels soft and sorry for someone, that is pity.

sentence.cn: 我们要怜悯弱小的动物。
sentence.en: We should have compassion for small, weak animals.

农.origin: The simple form of 農 — picture a farmer working the field with a hoe, growing our food.

sentence.en: The farmer grows vegetables in the field.

绅.origin: The silk sign 纟 on the left — a long silk sash tied at the waist, worn by a polite gentleman.

sentence.cn: 他是一位有礼貌的绅士。
sentence.en: He is a polite gentleman.
